description: A Flask-based inventory API with PostgreSQL, Redis, and an Nginx reverse proxy has been deployed at /home/ga/microservices-debug/ using Docker Compose. All four services have been started, but the application is not functioning correctly. Diagnose and fix all configuration issues so that: (1) curl http://localhost:80/api/health returns HTTP 200 with JSON containing "status": "healthy", "database": "connected", and "cache": "connected", (2) curl http://localhost:80/api/items returns HTTP 200 with a JSON array of inventory items from the database with id, name, and price fields, and (3) all four services (nginx, flask-app, db, redis) remain running. The Flask application source code (app/app.py) is correct and must NOT be modified. The issues are in the deployment configuration.
